_comment: Stable routing table: domain name -> sub-skill + how to detect it. Only coding-standards/SKILL.md is told this file exists -- no sub-skill's SKILL.md references it, so it functions as master-only without needing any permission.skill config. Update this when a new coding-standards-* sub-skill is added; the per-project 'which domains actually apply here' decision is NOT stored here -- that's re-detected fresh every session (see SKILL.md).

task_signals: building or modifying a page, component, modal, or drawer
project_signals: package.json dependency: react, svelte, vue, solid-js, or @angular/core
a components/, src/routes/, or src/lib/components/ directory

task_signals: adding or changing an API endpoint or middleware
project_signals: package.json dependency: express, hono, fastify, koa, or next (for API routes)
a src/routes/api/, src/server/, or api/ directory

task_signals: a schema change, migration, or database query
project_signals: a schema.zmodel, schema.prisma, or migrations/ file or directory
package.json dependency: prisma, drizzle-orm, or zenstack

task_signals: fetching, caching, or mutating server data from the client
explicit mention of TanStack Query, react-query, or an equivalent async-query/server-state library
writing or modifying end-to-end / Playwright tests
project_signals: a playwright.config.ts file, or a tests/ or e2e/ directory containing *.spec.ts files

task_signals: creating a new file and deciding where it belongs
scaffolding a new area of the project
general 'how do we structure X' questions
project_signals: always potentially applicable -- this domain is gated by the task signal, not a project signal
